title: Salim Kumar: the ultimate meme king
content: For three years after winning the National Award in 2011, the genial actor had stayed away from silver screen due to poor health. Yet he remained alive in public memory, thanks largely to memes that liberally drew on his image and witty dialogues, giving them a sharp satirical edge

title: After marathon talks, Congress convinces Ramalinga Reddy to stay in the Karnataka Cabinet
content: His decision to withdraw the resignation came after a series of meetings with D.K. Shivakumar, Randeep Singh Surjewala and B.K. Hariprasad

title: LPG price hiked by ₹29 per 14.2-kg cylinder
content: The increase follows a ₹60-per-cylinder hike on March 7 after the conflict in West Asia disrupted global energy supplies and drove up international fuel prices.

title: Versatile actor Salim Kumar passes away aged 57
content: He had been rushed to a private hospital with fever and shortness of breath in the early hours of June 6, and placed on ventilator support

title: Salim Kumar: a mimicry artiste par excellence before taking over the film arena
content: The art brought Salim Kumar fame and made him a sought-after performer in the famed Kalabhavan troupe and in foreign shows; it paved the way for his entry into the film industry

title: From ignominy, Salim Kumar took firm strides to national acclaim
content: In 1997, he was dropped from a film for his ‘lack of acting prowess.’ Thirteen years later, the National Film Award came his way through Adaminte Makan Abu. Salim Kumar, who has acted in 250-odd films, was a lively presence in the Malayalam film industry for three decades, switching effortlessly from comedy to character roles.

title: Driver held for illegal transport of river sand near Katpadi
content: The police said that the consignment was proceeding to Chengalpattu district for private contractors from Andhra Pradesh

title: District administration gears up for southwest monsoon preparedness in Vellore, nearby districts
content: Revenue officials said that the pre-monsoon summer showers had ensured significant widespread rain in these districts, especially in areas around Jawadhu Hills like Jolarpet and Yelagiri Hills in Tirupattur

title: Revenue staff seek adequate budget to meet routine requirements
content: They also call for establishment of a dedicated Revenue Academy to provide continuous training to enhance professional competence and improve public service delivery

title: History-sheeter arrested for assaulting man, son
content: Shyamkumar alias Thakaraparambu Kuttan, attacked Rajesh Kumar and his son, who questioned him for blocking the road

title: Scheduled Caste, minority wings of Congress plan joint stir against atrocities
content: MPs, MLAs,  and community representatives from across the country would participate in the July 20 rally, Rajendra Pal Gautam, head of the SC department, and Rajya Sabha member Imran Pratapgarhi, who heads the minority department, said at a press conference following a joint convention of the two departments.

title: NCP (SP) MP Sule vows to bring bill to stop horse trading in elections
content: Will move a bill in Parliament seeking structural changes to the electoral process; allegations of horse-trading during elections must stop, says NCP (SP) MP Supriya Sule

title: CJP founder applied for permission at Delhi airport, got it within minutes
content: After analysing the chatter on social media and getting a sense of the situation on the ground, it was felt that refusing permission may have led to some protesters storming the venue or the police station; the confrontation would have further fuelled the anger among the youth, government sources say

title: Amid global uncertainties, PM discusses measures to boost economic growth
content: Various reforms to improve ease of living and ease of doing business were also discussed, official sources said

title: State board students dominate CET toppers list
content: Among the top ten rank holders in all the seven streams, a majority of them are from the state board colleges

title: ‘There was no way to raise our grievances within Trinamool’, says Ritabrata Banerjee
content: Rebel Trinamool leader Ritabrata Banerjee says the grassroots party created by Mamata Banerjee has been hijacked by Abhishek Banerjee and corporate brokers; he rejects allegations that the BJP engineered the split, and reiterates that his faction will remain anti-BJP; he also hints at a possible rebellion among Trinamool MPs, saying ‘courage is contagious, it will travel from Kolkata to Delhi too’

title: India, Nepal can decisively shift trajectory of bilateral ties to achieve full potential: Jaishankar
content: Nepal wants to pursue “result-driven diplomacy” with India, its “most important partner”, says Nepal FM; “strong complementarities” between the current governments in Delhi, Kathmandu, says EAM

title: Memories, Medium, or Message? Art as mirror to a world in transition
content: The role of art institutions, how art must respond to a shifting socio-political landscape and its power to shape collective opinion were discussed

title: Four former AIADMK Ministers join TVK amid fresh wave of defections
content: M.C. Sampath, N.R. Sivapathi, Kadambur C. Raju, and Udumalai K. Radhakrishnan joined the party in the presence of Minister N. Anand

title: CJP warns of nationwide agitation if Education Minister Pradhan not removed in seven days
content: According to CJP spokespersons Ashutosh Ranka, removing the Education Minister was necessary if meaningful reforms were to be undertaken.

title: AI will reshape jobs, but India’s bigger challenge is preparing workers, boardrooms and classrooms
content: ‘You need humans to frame questions and inputs, AI does the work, and then you need humans again at the end to verify the outcome’

title: AIADMK to contest bypolls for 4 Assembly constituencies, says Edappadi K. Palaniswami
content: Speaking at a public meeting to thank the voters of his constituency of Edappadi, he alleges that the TVK is the same as the DMK and the people will soon understand about the TVK government

title: Arlekar, Vijay congratulate Praggnanandhaa for winning Norway Chess Tournament
content: In a post on X, the Chief Minister lauds Praggnanandhaa’s brilliant performance and wishes him success in his future endeavours

title: Third ro-ro ferry will be launched within a month, says Kochi Mayor
content: The new vessel will have the capacity to accommodate 14 trucks, 12 cars, and 50 passengers and has been designed to address the shortcomings of the existing ro-ro fleet

title: The Hindu Huddle | Manoj Bajpayee wants an image makeover
content: You cannot recreate human experience; the real-time performance is always authentic when compared to AI, says the actor in a light-hearted chat with Rohit Khilnani

title: The Hindu Huddle concludes with evocative musical performance by former diplomat Nirupama Rao
content: Ms. Rao, who is also the founder of South Asian Symphony Foundation (SASF), took to the stage in accompaniment with Sri Lankan pianist Soundarie David Rodrigo as the former envoy blended her experience of diplomacy with songs to end the event on a musical high

title: South Western Railway plans goods sheds along new lines to boost Karnataka’s regional economies
content: The initiative, being taken up by the South Western Railway, focuses on connecting production centres to markets

title: Police launch search for Guntur NRI family involved in job, visa fraud
content: “The couple has been absconding for the last few months and search has been launched for them,” said ACP N.V.S.K. Durga Rao, who is investigating the case

title: Attempts to sneak in political interests using Governor’s powers will be resisted, says Minister Roji M. John
content: Minister reiterates the government’s commitment to maintaining ideological integrity in the higher education sector while avoiding a direct confrontation with the Governor, which he warned could be detrimental to the higher education sector.

title: KWA, Kochi Corpn. to conduct joint inspection over alleged water contamination
content: Around 60 residents of the Kadavanthra, Panampilly Nagar and Perumanoor divisions reportedly complained of diarrhoea and vomiting after allegedly consuming drinking water supplied by the KWA

title: West Bengal Police recover large cache of arms hidden inside pond at Sandeshkhali
content: Videos of the recovery made from the waters of the pond by police personnel went viral on social media. Sandeshkhali had made headlines in early months of 2024, when locals, particularly women, came out in protests against the local Trinamool Congress leader Sheikh Shahjahan

title: Railways to invest ₹1 lakh crore in upgrading infrastructure in West Bengal
content: The Minister also said that the proposed bullet train services between Delhi–Varanasi and Varanasi–Siliguri were aimed at sharply reducing travel time

title: Trump urges new spy chief to fire employees
content: Bill Pulte, a Mr. Trump loyalist who heads the Federal Housing Finance Agency, was named by the Republican president on June 2 as acting Director of National Intelligence

title: MVD steps up safety checks of student carriers in Ernakulam
content: Around 70 vehicles were booked for various violations during the first week after schools reopened on June 2

title: Bihar BJP MLA convicted in case of celebratory firing that claimed woman’s life
content: The court directed that Raju Kumar Singh be taken into custody and scheduled arguments on sentencing for June 9

title: Regional parties better placed to respond to people’s needs: Former Speaker
content: “BRS’s resurgence is rooted in the fact that regional parties can respond better to the needs of people,” K.R. Suresh Reddy said

title: Regional parties assert independence, stress national interest
content: Rajya Sabha members Javed Ali Khan (Samajwadi Party), Santrupt Misra (Biju Janata Dal) and former BRS Parliamentary leader K.R. Suresh Reddy speak about raising issues of regional interest at the national level and the idea of holding simultaneous elections for both Parliament and State Assemblies

title: YSRCP leader Sajjala accuses A.P. govt. of depriving minorities of welfare
content: The current administration has failed to deliver on Haj assistance commitments, weakened the Minority Finance Corporation, and endangered Waqf properties while treating minorities as a vote bank, he says

title: How a murder in England by a Brit-Sikh triggered a MAGA meltdown over 'decline of western civilisation'
content: A British student's murder has ignited a transatlantic political firestorm. US Vice President JD Vance and Elon Musk have seized on the case, criticizing UK immigration and multicultural policies. This has sparked outrage in Britain, with accusations of American interference and a widening ideological rift between London and Washington.

title: 'For sake of world cricket': Ex-cricketer urges India to play Sooryavanshi vs Ireland
content: “I am glad he has been picked, for the sake of world cricket. He is the box-office man of the moment. I think he should play against Ireland. I was not entirely sure how good he was coming into this IPL," Swann said.

title: Meta's highest-paid employee’s 'health message' to Anthropic, OpenAI & Google
content: Meta's top AI executive, Alexandr Wang, revealed the company's strategy to challenge rivals like OpenAI and Google by focusing on health-related AI capabilities. While acknowledging current models aren't top-tier, Wang highlighted Meta's commitment to advancing AI for health applications, aiming to integrate these features into popular platforms like Facebook and Instagram.

title: Over 13L people join Annamalai’s 'We the Leaders' movement within 24 hours of its launch
content: Annamalai emphasised that the massive surge in registrations reflects a widespread public yearning for an alternative political paradigm in Tamil Nadu. He explicitly framed the launch not as an individual political ambition, but as a decentralised collective movement.

title: Bill proposes ending H-1B path to permanent residency and eliminating OPT program
content: U.S. Representative Chip Roy has introduced the "American White-Collar Worker Jobs Act of 2026" to reform the H-1B visa program. The bill aims to end H-1B visas as a path to permanent residency and eliminate the Optional Practical Training (OPT) program. It seeks to prioritize American STEM professionals by enforcing stricter wage standards and preventing displacement of U.S. workers.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet on one of the most-important lessons her dad taught her
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet revealed her father's advice after losing a speech contest shaped her career: 'You have to be so much better than everyone else, that they must pick you.' This lesson in merit, preparation, and self-belief, shared with Pfizer CEO Albert Bourla, continues to influence her ambition.  Bourla echoed this sentiment, emphasizing the danger of aiming too low.

title: J&K anti-terror operation: Army officer dies during military action in Rajouri
content: A tragic incident unfolded in Jammu and Kashmir's Rajouri district as a lieutenant lost his life after a fall into a deep gorge during an ongoing anti-terror operation. The officer was part of the extensive search for suspected Pakistani terrorists in the Dorimaal and Gambhir Mughalan areas. His body has since been recovered following the unfortunate accident.

title: India–Nepal ties: Jaishankar calls for ‘decisive shift’; Kathmandu signals reset
content: India and Nepal are poised to significantly enhance their "very special relationship," with discussions focusing on expanding cooperation in energy, digital technology, and new sectors. Both nations emphasized their civilizational ties and a shared resolve for a transformative partnership, aiming to unlock its full potential amidst ongoing collaboration and mutual support.

title: 'Deserved a chance to prove': Ashwin questions Suryakumar's India T20 snub
content: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) on Saturday announced India's T20I squads for the tours of England and Ireland, naming Shreyas Iyer as captain and Tilak Varma as vice-captain.

title: Connor McDavid car collection: Supercars, luxury garage & lifestyle
content: Connor McDavid’s luxury garage is still the hot ticket for hockey fans in 2026, and the Edmonton Oilers captain is said to own multiple luxury cars, including Porsche, Lamborghini, Audi, Mercedes-Benz and Land Rover in the lineup. McDavid’s car collection is a glimpse into the lifestyle of one of the NHL’s biggest stars, along with his ever-growing net worth and endorsement portfolio.

title: Mirra Andreeva net worth: Earnings, endorsements & lifestyle after French Open win
content: Mirra Andreeva is one of the brightest young stars in women’s tennis. The 19-year-old Russian has enjoyed a breakthrough rise on the WTA Tour. She has reached the French Open 2026 final. She has delivered impressive performances at Grand Slams and WTA 1000 events. These wins have made her one of the most talked-about players in and off tennis court.

title: Why Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are linked to a July MSG wedding
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are reportedly set to tie the knot at New York City’s Madison Square Garden on July 3, 2026. The choice is driven by unparalleled privacy. With plans for underground access and street closures to keep paparazzi at bay. The iconic venue also holds personal significance for Swift. She has performed multiple times there. As for Kelce’s connection, he was recently spotted for a Knicks vs. Cavaliers game at MSG with Swift.

title: 'Donald, Donald Trump': US prez posts self-praise anthem claiming world can't stop loving him
content: US President Donald Trump shared an AI-generated music video on Truth Social, celebrating his global admiration. The song, by Republican candidate Anthony Constantino, features exaggerated visuals of Trump in fantastical scenarios. Constantino expressed pride in Trump's endorsement of the track, which has garnered attention for its unique content and repetitive praise.

title: Patrick Mahomes and Brittany Mahomes combined net worth in 2026
content: Patrick Mahomes is embracing his quick recovery by enjoying sports with wife Brittany Mahomes. Their shared passion for sports extends to investments in various franchises. They co-own the Kansas City Current. The couple has been high school sweethearts. They have built a life together with three children and a luxurious Missouri mansion.

title: ‘Stood in the way’: Mother, boyfriend kill 6-year-old girl in Bengaluru
content: Police have reopened a three-month-old case of a six-year-old girl's mysterious death, now registering a murder charge against the victim's mother and her boyfriend. Initially attributed to a car AC incident, the father's suspicion, fueled by medical reports, led to a complaint alleging the child was beaten and smothered. The boyfriend has been arrested, while the mother remains at large.

title: 'People sitting abroad ... ': BJP chief issues warning in veiled swipe at CJP protest
content: BJP national president Nitin Nabin took a veiled dig at CJP for attempting to steer India's youth towards "negative politics." Speaking in Ranchi, he asserted that the nation's youth are focused on national development and securing their future, vowing to counter such attempts democratically. Nabin also emphasized the crucial role of farmers in India's progress.

title: 37-year-old Brazilian woman acts like 12, gets adopted, then arrested in 'multi-state fraud scheme'
content: A 37-year-old woman, Amanda Maria Souza de Oliveira, was arrested in Brazil for allegedly posing as a 12-year-old named "Gabriele." She is suspected of at least seven similar scams, using claims of medical conditions and childlike mannerisms to deceive families for up to 14 months.  Authorities linked her to cases across multiple states, noting a history of similar offenses.

title: Big blow to AIADMK: Over 300 members join TVK, cite inability to serve public effectively
content: Over 300 AIADMK members, including former ministers and MLAs, joined Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), citing inability to serve the public under AIADMK leadership. Senior leaders expressed belief in Chief Minister Joseph Vijay's ideals, mirroring MGR and Jayalalithaa, and pledged wholehearted support for TVK's future endeavors.

title: The fall of the twin flowers: How Mamata Banerjee lost control of her party
content: Barely a month after a crushing assembly defeat, Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool Congress faces its biggest test as a rebellion threatens to split the party. Fifty-eight of its 80 MLAs have rallied behind expelled leader Ritabrata Banerjee, who has been recognized as leader of the opposition, challenging the party's direction and Mamata's leadership.

title: The shadow fleet: How Iran keeps its oil flowing despite sanctions and war
content: Amidst Operation Epic Fury, a clandestine "shadow fleet" of aging tankers is enabling Iran and Russia to bypass sanctions and keep oil flowing. This hidden maritime network, utilizing deceptive tactics like disabled trackers and ship-to-ship transfers, allows sanctioned economies to earn billions, posing a significant challenge to global enforcement efforts and creating environmental risks.

title: ‘Discussed various issues’: Capt Amarinder Singh meets Shah, Nadda amid exit buzz
content: Senior BJP leader Capt Amarinder Singh met Union Home Minister Amit Shah and party chief JP Nadda amid speculation about his future. Singh had publicly criticized the appointment of Kewal Singh Dhillon as Punjab BJP chief, questioning his capability and expressing a lack of consultation. Rumors of a potential return to Congress persist, though BJP leaders dismiss them.

title: 'Look at the form': Agarkar's blunt explanation for Suryakumar T20 exit
content: Agarkar said Suryakumar's recent form was one of the factors behind the change, though not the only one.

title: NEET-UG re-test: NTA calls paper leak claims ‘false, fraudulent’, warns of strict action
content: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has firmly refuted social media claims of a NEET (UG) 2026 re-examination paper leak, calling them false and misleading. Asserting its commitment to a fair process, the NTA is lodging a complaint with cybercrime authorities.

title: French Open: Mirra Andreeva becomes first Russian woman since Maria Sharapova to win Slam
content: The victory made the 19-year-old Andreeva the youngest player to win the French Open women's singles title since Monica Seles, who was 18 when she claimed her third straight Roland Garros crown in 1992.

title: TMC turmoil: Minority cell secy quits over Abhishek Banerjee's 'dictatorial attitude'
content: TMC State Minority Cell Secretary Ajmal Siddiqui resigned, citing the party's disrepute and involvement in scandals. He criticized the leadership, particularly Abhishek Banerjee's "dictatorial attitude" and sycophancy, stating the party does little for the people. Siddiqui expressed a desire for development in Bengal, with no immediate plans to join another party.

title: Shreyas Iyer was 'close' to India's T20 World Cup squad, Agarkar drops bombshell
content: Shreyas Iyer has been appointed captain for India's upcoming T20 tours of Ireland and England, marking his return to the T20 setup. Teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi also earned a historic maiden call-up as the youngest player ever selected for an Indian team. This signals a new direction for India's T20 squad.

title: ‘Don't leave, we have PM Modi’: TN BJP chief amid mass resignations post Annamalai exit
content: Tamil Nadu BJP chief Nainar Nagendran urged party workers to stay put following K. Annamalai's exit and new movement launch. He asserted the BJP's strength, citing PM Modi, and dismissed concerns about a dent in the party's support base.  Despite several resignations, Nagendran maintained the BJP's robust future, emphasizing its ideology-driven nature.

title: Did cook's actions lead to Delhi hotel fire that killed 21? What probe says
content: A cook has been arrested in connection with the devastating south Delhi hotel fire that claimed 21 lives, with investigators suspecting his negligence may have sparked the blaze. This arrest follows the detention of other hotel staff as authorities probe safety violations and unauthorized construction.

title: 'Challenges ahead': Surya breaks silence after India snub as Iyer named captain
content: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) on Saturday named Shreyas Iyer as India's new T20I captain, replacing Suryakumar Yadav. Soon after the announcement, Suryakumar posted an Instagram story backing the team.

title: Delhi fire tragedy: In Hauz Rani, residents rely on ‘themselves, neighbours’ as fears linger
content: Hauz Rani grapples with the aftermath of a fatal fire, highlighting the pervasive danger of tangled overhead wires and inadequate infrastructure. Residents voice concerns about authorities only acting after tragedies, leaving them to rely on themselves in emergencies. The constant threat of electrical hazards is a grim reality locals have learned to endure.

title: 'God's child': Sooryavanshi gets his India moment after record-breaking IPL
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's childhood coach Manish Ojha says the 15-year-old's maiden India call-up is the result of talent, hard work and destiny. Fresh off a record-breaking IPL 2026 season, the Bihar teenager has earned praise from Sachin Tendulkar, Virat Kohli and Shubman Gill, and has now received a maiden India call-up.

title: Flowers in hand, calls for Pradhan's exit, 'cockroaches' end protest peacefully: All you need to know
content: The protest attracted hundreds of participants, most of them students and young professionals. Many attendees wore cockroach masks and carried flowers, while schoolchildren were also seen at the venue accompanied by their parents. Protesters raised slogans and called for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over issues linked to examinations such as NEET, CBSE, CUET and SSC.

title: Kohli replacement named for Afg ODIs, Agarkar delivers positive fitness update
content: Virat Kohli will miss the upcoming Afghanistan ODI series due to a hamstring injury sustained in the IPL final. Chief selector Ajit Agarkar confirmed the setback, stating Kohli might be fit for the England tour starting July 14. Yashasvi Jaiswal has been named as his replacement for the Afghanistan series, which begins June 13.

title: 'Hostile missiles, drones intercepted': Kuwait, Bahrain come under Iranian attack; watch
content: Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones toward Kuwait and Bahrain, prompting air defense responses in both nations. The US military intercepted Iranian missiles and drones targeting the Strait of Hormuz and allies, striking Iranian radar sites in response. These actions escalate tensions between Tehran and Washington amid ongoing regional confrontations.

title: TMC turmoil deepens: Sourav Ganguly denies asking Yusuf Pathan to resign on 'Mamata's behalf'
content: Sourav Ganguly has vehemently denied reports claiming he acted as an intermediary for Mamata Banerjee, conveying a message to Yusuf Pathan to resign from his elected post. Ganguly stated these allegations are untrue and made in reckless disregard of the truth, emphasizing his non-involvement in political matters.

title: IND vs AFG: KL Rahul sheds T20 edginess, Sudharsan makes the most of 'long rope'
content: KL Rahul's patient century and Sai Sudharsan's fluent 81 guided India to a strong position on Day 1 of the one-off Test against Afghanistan. Both batsmen successfully transitioned from T20 to Test cricket, overcoming initial rustiness and justifying the team management's faith. Their partnership laid a solid foundation for India's dominance.

title: Singapore Blocks Social Media Posts Inciting Racial Discord Against Indians
content: Singapore has long positioned its multicultural model - enshrined in law and national policy - as foundational to its stability and identity.

title: IIT Madras Team Takes India's Solar Car Dream To South Africa
content: For the first time, an Indian team will compete in the prestigious Sasol Solar Challenge in South Africa.

title: Chhattisgarh Man Travelling With Wife, Child Alleges Police Beat Him At Checkpoint
content: After verification, the man was allowed to leave and was instructed to appear at the police station the next day with his identity, vehicle and rental documents.

title: Fake Doctors Found In Madhya Pradesh, Purchased Degrees For Rs 10 Lakh
content: The racket was exposed first in Damoh, where three doctors posted under the National Health Mission at Sanjeevani Clinics were arrested.

title: India, Nepal Launch Cross Border Digital Payments, 84 Projects Handed Over
content: In a moment of both humanitarian and diplomatic significance, S Jaishankar virtually handed over 72 health facilities and 12 cultural heritage projects reconstructed with Indian development assistance following the devastating 2015 earthquake that killed nearly 9,000 people in Nepal.

title: 'India's Youth Not Puppets Of Some People On Foreign Soil': BJP Chief
content: "Some people, sitting abroad, sitting on foreign soil, think that they will give direction to the youth of India... make them puppets," BJP national chief Nitin Nabin said

title: From Rs 6,000 Salary To Rs 2 Crore In Assets: Odisha Engineer Under Scanner
content: The searches, carried out across Bhubaneswar, Balasore, Jajpur and Baliguda, have also uncovered multiple luxury properties, high-value land holdings and other financial assets. The operation is still underway.

title: 24 Hours After K Annamalai Leaves BJP, Nearly 14 Lakh Join His New Movement
content: Annamalai had left the civil services and joined the BJP in 2020. He was named state vice president within weeks of joining, and elevated to state unit chief just a year later, at the age of 37.

title: 'Undermined Under Abhishek Banerjee': Trinamool Minority Cell Leader Quits
content: In a letter to Trinamol Congress supremo Mamata Banerjee, Siddique wrote that the decision was not taken lightly as he had given his time.

title: Analysis: Operation Blue Star, An Emotional Memory, Not A Poll Issue
content: Recent elections suggest that while panthic issues continue to resonate with a segment of voters, they have not emerged as the dominant factor shaping electoral outcomes.

title: Delhi Infra Body Extends Booking Deadline For Karkardooma Housing Scheme
content: The scheme, launched on January 3, 2026, offers 741 two-bedroom (2-BHK) flats for individual buyers and an additional 107 flats reserved for bulk allotment to government institutions.

title: India's Currency Paradox: Cash Is King But Why ATMs Are Drying Up
content: After peaking at over 2.19 lakh machines in FY23, the number of ATMs has steadily declined to about 2.09 lakh in FY26.

title: Boyfriend Kills Pregnant Woman, Police Find Her Skeleton
content: The accused was later injured in an alleged encounter while attempting to flee from police custody, police said.

title: Adani Enterprises Plants 1.6 Million Trees To Turn Chhattisgarh Coal Mine Green
content: Under the programme, 40 trees are planted for every tree removed for mining activities. Native species including Sal, Mahua, Tendu, Amaltas and Sidha have been replanted

title: Brother, Nephew Accused Of Killing NRI Over Property In Amritsar
content: According to the police, the prime accused, Satish Sharma, allegedly conspired with his son, Sunny Sharma, and property dealer Lakhwinder Singh to eliminate Sunil Sharma.

title: 2 Men Drown In River While Swimming And Making Reels In UP's Gorakhpur
content: The incident took place near the Kartahari village under the Pipiganj police station area, where a group of seven friends had gone to bathe in the river.

title: No Women In US Navy Promotion List Raises Fears Of Career Ceiling
content: The Navy had selected 31 sailors to promote from the rank of captain to one-star admiral, but Hegseth recently intervened to strike nine people from the list, including three women and two Black men, according to a defence official.

title: 7-Month-Old Palestinian Baby Killed After Israeli Troops Shoot At Family Car
content: Abu Haikal said the family was travelling with their 11-year-old son and his mother when the shooting took place. He rejected any suggestion that the soldiers could have mistaken the vehicle's occupants.

title: 13.5 Million Km, 80,000 Words: Shubhanshu Shukla Brings Space Down To Earth
content: Shukla's transition from astronaut to author comes on the back of a landmark mission. During the Axiom-4 Mission, the crew of four travelled a total of approximately 13.5 million kilometres and completed 320 orbits of Earth.

title: The bird that woke us up has fallen silent. Can we bring back the Sparrow's chirp?
content: the song of the birds is no longer the soundtrack of dawn . the sparrow population has decreased by nearly 64% in Europe since the 1970s . birdlife international reports that the population has dropped by nearly 6% in Europe .

title: $176,000 for a NBA final ticket: How watching sports became a luxury pastime
content: the golden age of entertainment ticketing has become less a leisure activity and more a wealth-management decision . the return of the NY Knicks to the NBA Finals after a 27-year drought has turned Madison Square Garden into the world's most expensive temporary real estate . one pair of courtside tickets reportedly sold for nearly $280,000 (approx Rs 2.6 crores)

title: 5 cops suspended for Pocso case manipulation in Samba
content:
.

JAMMU: Cracking down on lapses in the handling of crimes against children, the SSP of J&K’s Samba district suspended the SHO of Ramgarh police station, a sub-inspector, two ASIs, and a munshi over allegations of facilitating a compromise in a Pocso case. They have been attached to police lines pending an investigation into the charges. “This development comes as part of Samba police’s ongoing efforts to ensure accountability and transparency. SSP Anuj Kumar, who assumed charge earlier this year, has been emphasising strict action against any misconduct by police personnel,” an official said. In a related development, a special police officer has been transferred from Ramgarh police station to another police post.

End of Article

title: Sriram Krishnan, Trump's Indian-origin AI brain, to exit White House
content: Sriram Krishnan will leave his post at the end of the month . the Indian-born immigrant helped shape the Trump administration's artificial intelligence strategy . he became one of the most influential voices inside the white house . Krishnan said he will take a break before launching a new initiative .
